reviews.ownerResponse:
A lawyer’s duty to keep client confidences has few exceptions and in an abundance of caution I do not feel at liberty to respond in a point-by-point fashion in this forum. Suffice it to say that I do not believe that the post presents a fair and accurate picture of the events. My office always strives to satisfy all clients, even though I am well aware that is not always possible for a multitude of reasons including unreasonable expectations or failure of clients to cooperate in their legal representation.

Fortunately, or Unfortunately I am not allowed to respond to the specifics of this review due to very specific ethical duties imposed on me as an attorney. Social media also creates a potential risk of disclosing (inadvertently or otherwise) privileged or confidential information, including the identities of current or former clients. The duty to protect privileged and confidential client information extends to current clients (RPC 1.6), former clients (RPC 1.9), and prospective clients (RPC 1.18). Consistent with these rules, ABA Formal Opinion 10-457 provides that lawyers must obtain client consent before posting information about clients on websites. In a content-driven environment like social media where users are accustomed to casually commenting on day-to-day activities, including work-related activities, lawyers must be especially careful to avoid posting any information that could conceivably violate confidentiality obligations.

I came into the office the day before court and was looking at losing everything! Sharon tate and her team turned it around in no time and won more then expected for me! Before getting a lawyer i highly suggest talking to her and her team to see what they can do for you. Thank you guys again!

This was not an amazing experience, it was costly, it was nerve wrecking, but I do understand the cost, they got staff, court filing, hours and days put into just one case. This is a problem with the system, that those with no funds, are rarely seeing the same justice they deserve. But let be clear, I am fully and wholeheartedly in my appreciation of this law firm, from the interaction of the paralegal, to the talks and court hearing with Mrs Tate. She came recommended from a friend, and she definitely lived up to that recommendation.
If you need an attorney call Sharon Tate and staff! They treat you like a treasured friend and fight for you like family. You will never feel like just another number.

reviews.ownerResponse:
We understand that any family law case can be extremely taxing and stressful. Our intention is always to get you through it as cost-effectively and stress-free as possible.

As with all law firms, all time spent on a case is billable. It appears that your true complaint was that we billed you to do work that was necessary. It's unfortunate that it does cost money to have legal representation to deal with complex family law matters. We are here to provide that service, but it is not free of charge.

Our firm adheres to a strict code of professional ethics, billing only for work done on behalf of our clients consistent with the written attorney-client fee agreement each client carefully reviews and signs when they retain us. Additionally, every client receives a detailed accounting of all work done on their case as it progresses.

We are here to help our clients and do that in the most cost-effective way possible, but there are certain things that must be done (and billed for) to begin and end a case. We hope you understand, and if you would like to further discuss this, please feel free to contact us.

reviews.text:
My experience with the Mova Law Group was nothing short of phenomenal.  Chris Mova’s team is truly dedicated to providing excellent service, making me feel confident that my case was in the best possible hands.  I am thoroughly impressed by the exceptional legal services they provide. From the moment I spoke to Chris Mova, I was greeted with professionalism and genuine care, attributes that set this firm apart from others.
Chris is a skilled negotiator who possesses an impressive depth of knowledge and expertise and is a staunch advocate for his clients to navigate complex legal matters without any difficulty.  Chris was outstanding in handling my personal injury case with his attention to detail and providing clear, concise guidance that is nothing short of remarkable. After my car accident, I was unaware of my legal rights and Chris went above and beyond to help me understand my options, ensuring I received first-rate medical attention while attending to the legal aspects of my case.
What truly distinguishes the Mova Law Group from others is their unwavering commitment to their clients. They prioritize understanding the specific needs and circumstances of each client, ensuring that their approach is personalized and effective. Throughout my interactions, all of the team members at Mova Law Group listened intently to my needs with empathy which proved to be an invaluable support because I knew that my best interests were always at the forefront.
Mova Law Group also provided me with regular updates on the progress of my case, checking in on my well-being, especially under difficult circumstances. This transparency and accessibility fostered trust and confidence, making me feel at ease during what can often be a stressful legal process.  Ultimately, the success of Mova Law Group can be measured by the results they achieve. Chris and his team have worked tirelessly on my case, always going the extra mile to secure the best possible results.
In sum, the Mova Law Group’s dedication to my case resulted in a settlement that far superseded my expectations.  Chris and his team exemplify the highest standards of legal practice. I highly recommend the Mova Law Group because of their expertise, professionalism, and strong commitment to their clients, making them an outstanding choice for anyone seeking personal injury legal assistance.

reviews.text:
I do not recommend Rebecca Rainwater for child custody cases.  I paid the retainer for her for my daughter in a custody case with my granddaughter.  I also consulted with her.   The attorney told my daughter on Saturday before the Monday hearing that she would be attending by phone and would be at another case in another town at the same time. (My guess is that this allowed her to charge her other case travel time and at the same time she charged my daughter for the wait time as she waited on hold on the phone for two hours for the hearing, mostly likely while doing other work.) She also told my daughter she would ask for a continuance (delay) because the other party had not filed all the paperwork.   She showed up by phone, but did not even ask for the continuance.  Her reason she gave later was ridiculous.  She said it was because he hadn't filed the paperwork. Huh?

She does not pay attention to detail. In spite of being told, she continued to misspell the name of the opposing party.

She had agreed to ask for some changes in the holiday/special days schedule.  Instead of having these requested changes filed in writing ahead of time prepared for the judge to read, she tried to just discuss the details on the phone during the hearing.  It was too confusing for everyone with nothing in writing, so the judge made no changes. Ms. Rainwater did not bring up the majority of items that she had agreed to address at the hearing or push back on most of the oppositions’ requests.  The opposition got 8 hours of visitation time when he is not even home.  After the hearing, she did not get a copy of the orders.  We kept asking for the orders for a week and a half and she kept saying the orders were not in the court yet, but the other party had them and were giving us grief because we weren’t following them.  Turned out the opposing lawyer had been assigned by the judge to write them up, but our lawyer just kept checking the court file and charging us for the time to check.  The court would give no information because we had a lawyer, so we couldn't do it ourselves.
She stated what a caring person the other grandmother was in a text to my daughter.  How she would know that I have to wonder and what did that matter anyway.  It was like she was on the other side. When we asked her why she said positive things about the other grandmother, she said she didn't want to alienate the judge.  Does the judge read her texts too? She talks a good talk on the phone ahead of time, but refuses to see you in peson or show up in person, overcharges, and then does not do what she said she would do.
reviews.ownerResponse: I’m sorry I know it’s hard when a judge does not align with a parent’s desires in a custody situation.it would be inappropriate to go into specific details on social media. It is unfortunate that neither you or your daughter raised any of these concerns while we were on our call and I explained that I got your daughter what she asked me to obtain, however the Judge added an overnight to father and that was within his discretion to make that order. I know it’s hard to share a child  when the parents can not get along. I wish you both well.

reviews.text:
Hiring This Attorney Was a Nightmare – You’ve Been Warned!

Hiring Rebecca Rainwater was one of the most frustrating and disappointing professional experiences I’ve ever had. The complete lack of communication, professionalism, and basic client service was beyond unacceptable.

1️⃣ Unresponsive & Disrespectful: Reaching out was like talking to a wall. Calls and emails went ignored for unreasonable periods, leaving me constantly chasing basic information. This is not just poor service, it’s outright negligence.

2️⃣ Inexcusable Delays: Critical updates and documents were delayed for weeks, causing unnecessary stress and confusion. There’s no excuse for this level of disorganization.

3️⃣ No Guidance or Support—I expected professional advice and direction. What I got was silence and indifference, leaving me to handle important decisions on my own. If you want an attorney who will abandon you, look no further.

4️⃣ Zero Billing Transparency: Not once did I receive a bill until my retainer was completely drained. How they can justify draining client funds without providing a single detailed invoice is beyond me. This is shady and unacceptable.

Bottom line: Save yourself the frustration and money. Working with Rebecca Rainwater was a nightmare I wouldn’t wish on anyone. Their lack of professionalism is appalling, and I’m taking steps to ensure no one else has to endure what I did. Do yourself a favor—look elsewhere.

Working with Colleen and her team has been a pure joy. They are very professional.  Colleen made the Trust process easy to understand and worked within my time frame. I love working all of them. I retained the services of Brad Landon approximately 5 years ago, I did so after my previous attorney was no longer in alignment with my goals.

Sadly, I have had to have the services of a family law attorney for a large part of my adult life, and while many people using the services of an attorney, wish that they didn't need to; when you do need one- you want the best.

Brad isn't the best because he's ruthless, it's exactly the opposite- he is able to navigate through times that are emotionally painful for both parties, and brings a sense of civility that is sometimes lost when dealing with family law issues.

I respect and trust his professional opinion, and it has more than once guided me from making what would have been an emotionally charged decision and not in my best interests.

reviews.text: I had the pleasure of having Brett represent me in my vehicle accident case with the assistance of Stacey. My case was a tough one and the other party really went to great lengths to fight Brett at every turn. They drug us out for almost a year and im sure would of attempted to take it even farther but Brett went to BAT. This man represented us to the fullest extent in every way we needed. He was always very concise and explained every outcome of every choice we had to make. We never felt left in the dark or like we weren't in full control of our situation. When we were mad, he was mad with us, when we needed a friend, he was there to put our hearts at ease, when we needed to fight, he pushed us to the finish lines. Brett went out of his way multiple times in so many aspects we always felt like he wasn't just our lawyer but our friend, and eventually, he became family. It never felt like it was "just about the money" which you can run into so often but he also never gave up on making sure we got our money! He fought so hard for us we actually settled above what we were hoping for. He genuinely cared when our children were suffering from the accident and so many times reached out just to make sure we were physically and emotionally okay. Stacy does an excellent job meeting deadlines, responding quickly, and ensuring its done right the first time. As a team, they really are a golden star for this firm and all clients. Even though we truly deserved the world after our accident, we left the situation feelings so grateful and wonderfully represented. We will recommend Brett and Stacy time and time again and should we ever need legal representation later in life, Brett is our first call. We truly can't thank him enough for being the lawyer his is and the wonderful person he is as well.
reviews.ownerResponse: Tyler, thank you for taking the time to review our team and your experience. Bret and Stacey truly go the extra for their clients. We are proud to have them on our team. Thank you for being a wonderful client, take care!

reviews.text: When my daughter was hit on a scooter by a speeding vehicle, I was mortified! Her uninsured motorist policy denied payment and the driver of the vehicle that hit her had no insurance.  A friend of mine who works with John said they could help so I gave the info to my daughter and she set up an appointment. Soon after they started the ball rolling. My mom and I took shifts taking care of Monika during the day.  She was in a wheelchair that made it very difficult in her small house. She had surgery’s and then rehab and another surgery.  I watched my daughter hurt and cry and I felt helpless, and when she lost her job I watched her get angry! But, I also watched  a team of people go to work fighting for her HARD!  They won her case and the insurance paid, more than I imagined she was able to pay medical bills, and the outstanding bills that had come with not working.  I would highly recommend Gomez Trial Attorneys to everyone, they actually care about people and will fight hard to take care of them

Dante, I truly appreciate your 5-star review and the trust you placed in me to handle your DUI case at the Southwest Justice Courthouse. Getting arrested for a DUI in Murrieta or Temecula can be overwhelming, but I want everyone reading this to know—there are always options. No matter the circumstances of your case, I have the experience and skill to fight for the best possible outcome and protect your future.

DUI charges can have serious consequences, especially when they involve injuries (VC 23153), drugs instead of alcohol, multiple prior offenses, or refusals to take a breathalyzer or field sobriety tests. The prosecution is aggressive in these cases, but I know exactly how to challenge the evidence, attack the police report, and negotiate favorable deals to minimize penalties.

At the Southwest Justice Courthouse, I regularly handle cases involving:

✅ DUI Causing Injury (VC 23153) – When an accident occurs, prosecutors push for felony charges with harsh penalties. I work to prove lack of impairment, challenge blood alcohol results, and negotiate for a reduction to a wet reckless or misdemeanor DUI.

✅ DUI on Drugs (DUID) – Unlike alcohol DUIs, there is no clear legal limit for drugs, which means the evidence is highly subjective. I know how to challenge toxicology reports, police observations, and faulty Drug Recognition Expert (DRE) testimony to weaken the case against my clients.

✅ Third Offense DUI (VC 23152) – A third DUI in Murrieta or Temecula means mandatory jail time, a long license suspension, and a possible 18-month DUI program. I fight hard to reduce jail time, negotiate alternative sentencing like house arrest, and push for restricted license options to keep my clients driving.

✅ DUI Refusals – Refusing a breathalyzer or field sobriety tests can result in automatic DMV suspensions and harsh court penalties. But these cases also have defenses, especially if the officer failed to properly advise you of your rights or coerced the refusal. I know how to fight these charges and get them reduced or dismissed.

🚗 DMV License Suspensions & Restricted Licenses 🚗

A DUI isn’t just about the court case—the California DMV can suspend your license even if your criminal case is dismissed. That’s why I always fight both the DMV hearing and the court charges. I have helped countless clients in Temecula and Murrieta keep their driving privileges by securing:

✅ Set-asides (DMV dismissals of suspensions)
✅ Restricted licenses that allow driving to work/school
✅ Ignition interlock device (IID) options to regain full driving privileges

🏛 Jail Time, Fines & Probation – How I Fight for My Clients 🏛

Many people think a DUI means automatic jail time, but that’s not always the case. At the Southwest Justice Courthouse, I have successfully:

✅ Reduced jail time to house arrest or electronic monitoring
✅ Negotiated fines down to the minimum amount possible
✅ Secured informal probation to keep clients out of custody
✅ Avoided mandatory DUI programs for certain clients

I know how to navigate the complex DUI laws in California and push for the best possible outcome. In many cases, I have secured wet reckless deals, which carry less severe penalties than a full DUI conviction.

🔥 Why I’m the Best DUI Defense Attorney in Temecula & Murrieta 🔥

For 18 years, I have defended thousands of DUI cases, including felony DUIs, drug DUIs, and refusals. I know the Southwest Justice Courthouse system inside and out and have built a reputation for getting results where others don’t. When you hire me, you’re getting an attorney who:

✔ Knows how to challenge DUI evidence and test results
✔ Aggressively fights to reduce jail time and license suspensions
✔ Has extensive experience with DMV hearings and restricted licenses
✔ Negotiates wet reckless deals whenever possible
✔ Is a top-rated DUI attorney with a proven track record of success

For anyone reading this who is facing DUI charges in Temecula, Murrieta, or the Southwest Justice Courthou

Dear Margaret,

Thank you so much for your thoughtful 5-star review! It truly means the world to us to hear about your positive experience with our legal team. Facing criminal charges is never easy, and we know how stressful and overwhelming the legal process can be. We are honored that you trusted us to defend your case, and we are thrilled that we could achieve a successful outcome for you.

From the very beginning, we knew that your case required a strong and strategic defense. The criminal justice system in Murrieta, Temecula, and throughout Riverside County can be intimidating, especially for those who have never been through it before. That’s why we made it our priority to ensure that you felt supported, informed, and confident in your defense every step of the way. The Southwest Justice Center in Murrieta is known for handling a variety of criminal cases, and we are proud to have extensive experience navigating the courts there on behalf of our clients.

When we first met, we could sense the anxiety and concern you had about your case. It’s completely understandable—criminal charges can impact every aspect of a person’s life, from their career and reputation to their personal relationships. That’s why we take every case seriously, ensuring that no detail is overlooked in our pursuit of justice. From analyzing the evidence to challenging the prosecution’s claims, we left no stone unturned in building the strongest possible defense for you.

Every criminal case is unique, and we believe that a personalized approach is essential to achieving the best outcome. We thoroughly reviewed every aspect of your situation, examined the evidence, and identified any weaknesses in the prosecution’s case. Whether it was questioning law enforcement procedures, challenging witness statements, or negotiating favorable terms, we fought tirelessly to ensure that your rights were fully protected.

Having handled numerous cases at the Southwest Justice Center in Murrieta, we understand the nuances of the local court system, the judges, and the prosecution’s approach. This experience allowed us to anticipate challenges and develop an effective strategy that ultimately led to a positive resolution in your case. We know that the stakes are high in criminal defense matters, which is why we are committed to providing aggressive, knowledgeable, and compassionate legal representation for every client we serve.

Beyond just the legal process, we also understand the emotional toll that criminal charges can take. Our goal is not only to provide strong legal advocacy but also to offer reassurance, guidance, and support during what can be an incredibly difficult time. We want every client to feel heard, respected, and empowered throughout their case, and we are so glad that you felt that level of care and dedication from our team.

Your trust in us means everything, and your willingness to share your experience is a reminder of why we do what we do. Defending our clients is more than just legal work—it’s about helping good people navigate tough situations and move forward with their lives. Seeing you come out on the other side of this with a positive outcome is the most rewarding part of our job.

If you ever need legal assistance again, whether it’s for yourself or someone you care about, please don’t hesitate to reach out. We take great pride in serving the communities of Murrieta, Temecula, and the surrounding areas, and we remain committed to fighting for justice on behalf of those who need it most.

Once again, Margaret, thank you for your kind words, your trust, and the opportunity to represent you. We wish you nothing but the best moving forward, and if you ever need anything, you know where to find us.

Darcie, thank you for your incredible 5-star review. It means so much to me and my team at the Law Office of Bradley Corbett. We understand how stressful and overwhelming it can be to face domestic violence charges, and I’m truly honored that you trusted me to fight for you.

Domestic violence cases are often complex, emotional, and highly charged. When the police respond to a domestic disturbance call, they frequently make arrests based on minimal evidence, often with little more than an accusation. In many cases, there are misunderstandings, exaggerated claims, or even false allegations made out of anger or during a contentious divorce or custody battle. Unfortunately, law enforcement officers are trained to make an arrest if there is even a hint of physical altercation, regardless of whether the evidence supports the claim.

In your case, like so many others, the arrest led to immediate consequences. Many people don’t realize that when someone is arrested for domestic violence, the police often request an Emergency Protective Order (EPO) that can force them out of their own home and prevent contact with their spouse or partner for several days. This can be devastating, especially for couples who share children, a home, or financial responsibilities. One of the first steps I take in cases like these is to challenge these emergency orders and work to prevent a longer-term restraining order from being issued.

Domestic violence cases at Southwest Justice Center, Temecula, Murrieta, Fallbrook, and even the Pala Indian Reservations are prosecuted aggressively. The district attorney’s office often moves forward with charges even if the alleged victim doesn’t want to press charges. That’s why having a skilled attorney on your side from the very beginning is critical. I always take the time to investigate every angle, reviewing police reports, body cam footage, witness statements, and medical records to identify weaknesses in the prosecution’s case.

One of the biggest challenges with domestic violence cases is the lack of sufficient evidence to prosecute. Many cases rely solely on the statements of one party, without any physical injuries, witnesses, or other proof. The prosecution often assumes that if an arrest was made, there must be a case. However, I know how to expose weak and baseless accusations. I challenge inconsistencies, cross-examine witnesses, and demand the prosecution prove their case beyond a reasonable doubt.

For many of my clients, avoiding a conviction is critical to their future. A domestic violence conviction can have severe consequences, including jail time, probation, mandatory domestic violence classes (DV classes), loss of gun rights, and immigration consequences for non-citizens. In cases like yours, I worked tirelessly to negotiate a dismissal or reduction of charges to prevent long-term damage to your record.

But when the prosecution refuses to drop or reduce charges, I am never afraid to take a case to trial. I have successfully defended clients in jury trials, exposing weak cases and getting not guilty verdicts when the evidence simply wasn’t there. Many attorneys try to push plea deals to avoid trial, but I am always prepared to fight in court when necessary. My reputation as a strong trial attorney gives me leverage in negotiations because the prosecution knows I won’t back down.

Your case at Southwest Justice Center was a perfect example of how a strong defense can make all the difference. By conducting a thorough investigation, presenting evidence in your favor, and aggressively challenging the allegations, we were able to get the best possible outcome.

I take pride in the work I do, not just because of the legal victories but because of the lives I help rebuild. A domestic violence arrest can be humiliating and life-changing, but with the right defense, it doesn’t have to define your future. That’s why I am always available for my clients, guiding them through every step, answering questions
Thanks Kim!

reviews.text:
I am pleased to share my experience with Nic Cocis, who represented my son in a very challenging legal situation. Last year, my son was involved in a serious car accident while driving to a pool hall on game night, which resulted in significant injuries and felony charges. Faced with up to six years in prison, we were desperate to find the right attorney.

After extensive research and interviews with various lawyers, we found Nic Cocis, and it was one of the best decisions we could have made. Nic’s has a solid 5.0 rating and its well-deserved. From the outset, he was incredibly kind, attentive, and responsive to all forms of communication. His calm demeanor and exceptional communication skills reassured us throughout the process.

Nic’s preparation was meticulous. He gathered comprehensive evidence, including pictures, character letters, and my son’s personal background life resume, to build a compelling case. His ability to present my son's story with such depth and empathy was impressive. In the courtroom, Nic's performance was outstanding—his handling of the case was as captivating as watching a rock star in concert.

Thanks to Nic’s expertise and dedication, we were able to negotiate a 3-year probation deal, a significant reduction from the initial charges. This was my son's first DUI and his first encounter with legal issues. We are immensely grateful to Nic for his hard work and exceptional representation. Thank you, Nic!.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for your 5-star review! It was a pleasure working with you and your son, and I’m glad we could achieve a positive outcome for him. Wishing you all the best!

reviews.text:
I was referred to this firm specifically for representation by Mr. Bradford, but unfortunately, my experience was extremely disappointing. Throughout my case, I was transferred between multiple attorneys and never received consistent representation. Despite being told that Mr. Bradford would personally handle my case, he never did. Instead, I spoke with three different attorneys during the process, and a different attorney was sent to represent me on my court date.

This constant reassignment made it feel as though my case was not taken seriously and was simply being passed around within the firm. Communication was inconsistent, and I did not feel supported or properly advocated for.

Additionally, the firm charges high fees for nearly every interaction, with hourly rates exceeding $500, which did not reflect the level of service provided.

I chose this firm based on a recommendation, but had I known this would be my experience, I would not have hired them. I would strongly encourage others to carefully consider their options and explore other firms before committing.

Mr. Bradford took my case 4 years ago, and today we finally closed out my file.

I appreciate this man more than I can say. Every time we appeared in court, I was blown away with his preparation, professionalism and familiarity with every detail of my case.

He always showed up with a massive briefcase, and at one point I remarked, "wow that's a lot of cases." He said, "Nope, this is all you." Despite how lengthy and complicated the case was, any time we were in court he would very quickly be able to flip to pertinent documents even from years ago and point out evidence to the judge.

I kid you not, after one of our hearings, I even witnessed another person who was so impressed with Bradford that he approached him afterward; I assume with intent to retain his services. It's just that obvious to everyone how competent this man is, and you can tell the courts respect him for his professionalism and knowledge of the law.

Thank you Erik!

This is the best law firm I've ever dealt with.  Every person who works here make the effort to help you in any way they can!!!
The team of lawyers is unmatched and are the best I have ever dealt with.  The paralegals are subject matter experts and easy to talk to.  The office staff are amazing and always friendly.

Thank you all so much for what you do!!!

These attorneys helped us for a divorce in our family. Not only did they clearly not read any materials before court but they did nothing to help represent us. They are the worst attorneys. Two times they came to court barely knowing our names and the bill was $7000! Please save yourself and find another attorney!!

Constant charges for useless communication. Every email with a miniscule update cost me a few hundred dollars. I am taking my ex back to court with a new lawyer and have some much better results coming forth. They were much more interested in passing the buck and going to the gym. 2 stars because he did come up with a decent idea about how to split alimony and child support. $40k+ for a two star result. I would check elsewhere before committing to this group.
Call these guys for any civil or family matters you may have. Great experience. Thank you Mr. Bradford and team.
Great customer service. I’m a local realtor in Temecula and I trust they’ll get the job done.

I am a previously divorced man.
I did not know of Shaun during my initial proceedings and ended up selecting someone else.
During my original experience, I suppose I felt what many people going through a divorce feel….hopelessness; anxiety over financials, etc.  (my kids were grown so luckily I didn’t have custody issues).
At any rate, let me just say that I was heavily involved in defending my cause and ended up with a fair deal.  The reason I share this is because years later I needed representation again.
Although my formal relationship with my previous attorney ended, I tried reaching out to him to no avail.  We had one live conversation and then he ghosted me afterward during a time when I needed representation and reassurance.
I met a gentleman in the gym I go to, and he told me about Shaun.  He knew someone who Shaun represented and had nothing but positives to share.
He gave me his number and I reached out that day.  Literally in minutes, I get a call back from Shaun.
I was forthright about my situation and he immediately put my mind at ease.
He reassured me everything would be ok and accurately described how the process would work, to a “T”.
Shaun and his team had my situation resolved within weeks for a fair price.
Many people want an attorney who are fighters! I wanted someone who had a strong presence, but understood the art of the deal.
Fighters end up creating long and drawn out cases with tons of discovery and cost a ton of money with no end in sight.
Deal makers get things done, that saves everyone money!
Shaun’s reputation in the valley is well respected.  He understands and leverages his relationships within his attorney network to get things done, while making you feel protected and taken care of.
From personal experience, I will only refer Shaun for those seeking assistance.
I’ve read some of the other reviews and frankly, those comments are in stark contrast to what I experienced, and I’ve experienced more than Shaun.
If you’re lucky enough to get him and his team, you won’t be disappointed!

Ex wife’s attorney. Lies curses dont respond to any emails or calls … violates so many court rules. Made two complaints to the bar and riverside court house. Looking to connect with others who have had similar experience with this woman   I feel bad for my ex wife who is getting so overcharged this woman is so unprofessional curses and  just refuses to communicate even at the judges orders.

Michelle Brooker is Top Notch! Shes the "Camille Vasquez" we all need in our lives when going through our toughest moments. She is so reasonable and tough when needed.

I highly recommend this office to anyone who has been dealing with Riverside County Court and needs to obtain custody of their children.  After speaking with several offices,  Danica Hanich carefully listened to my family law situation.  They did not over promise and they did deliver. After 7 years of continuous abuse, and the court overlooking the abuse as detrimental to my child, I finally obtained custody of my child with the help of this office. Words cannot express how thankful I am that I can now get my child what she needs. Mr. Hanson and Mr. Bradford represented me on 2 occasions and both were very professional. They were prepared for each court date. This office was always responsive to me and it was a great sense of relief knowing that this office was handling what needed to be handled. I only wish I had contacted them sooner. Thank you all for your help!

I do not recommend them to anyone. These are peoples lives and their families that people are fighting for and the office did not care. They over promised and undelivered on every matter. They committed to things and lied, just to leave the client in the worst position. They don’t care and they take you money & leave you with a sense of loss. I do not recommend them to anyone. You can’t trust who is fighting for you and your family at this office. Do Better Hansen

I have never came across a law firm that has shown more care and dedication as this firm. All experiences with employees has been wonderful. They are responsive and really show you they have your best intentions at heart. They are so knowledgeable and walked in to that court room like a shark ready to take down whatever came at them. When I thought I had no chance they gave me my leg up. I appreciate all they have done for me and will continue to use them forever. I can’t say enough good things about the entire staff. Thank you.
Over priced . And provide bare minimum service

Chuck Gorian is the best! He was really helpful in regards to answering my questions and not giving me the boot to refer me to other attorneys without giving me some feedback. Other attorney offices I had spoken to told me they wouldn’t be able to help me or that I could find somewhere cheaper without even giving me any kind of consultation or feedback. He made my day and helped me to the best of his ability and it was very sincere and genuine and he actually gave me some hope. I would HIGHLY RECOMMEND this office, he fights for his clients and is knowledgeable and is a very caring individual. 10 stars out of 5 if I could!

They are absolutely the worst lawyers around. If your case is at all important to you please keep scrolling and call a different lawyer! I am so devastated with the ruling I got because of them it is absolutely ruining my life every single day.  For the love of God if your case is at all important to you call a different lawyer!  Especially Michelle Broker!  I was supposed to get Hanson and instead I got the biggest man hater who didn't believe an honest word I said.      I can't write enough bad reviews. I would've rather have represented myself and it would have saved me 5000 they lied about their price and they weren't even worth a dollar.

This ma

This law firm is only in it for the money and how much money they can get out of you. This firm also charges for everything , and seems like they stretch out the process so they can charge more on your case.

Worst lawyers in town.  They over charged me like crazy.  The lawyer I had was against me the whole time, they didn't have my case ready because they started reviewing it the day before court causing a continuance and way more money.  They couldn't get a absolutely bogus restraining order removed and charged me $5000 for doing nothing for me!! Worst choice in my life to use them... Please if your case is important to you call someone else!!!

They had my best interest in mind and not just my money. They communicated well and where up front and honest about my case. At the end of the day we walked away from a victory that I would not have gotten without them. I trust and recommend this firm.

Just look at the other reviews, definitely look elsewhere. No communication whatsoever and when you email or call them to find out what’s going on with your case, YOU get charged when it should be them letting you know what’s going on !. Stay away.....
Just got off the phone with Chuck Gorian. He was very knowledgeable and helpful with my situation. Thank you!

Divorce is so stressful, but with their skilled team and amazing lawyers, I got everything I was asking for. They were incredibly helpful. My ex's lawyer was like putty in Mr. Hanson's hands. Highly recommend!

I could not have asked for a more caring and yet really professional attorney to fight for me. I was pregnant when I first started this whole custody process with my ex husband so a was very emotional and stressed out. After the first consultation with her I knew she was the one. She talked to me like a person not just like another case number. She always had my childrens best interest in mind and always spoke truthfully about the process and expectations. Everyone at her current office was always very friendly and supportive. The paralegal in my case, Monica Clapp, was the best. The communication was ALWAYS done very promptly. If and when there was something new I was notified almost immediately. Attorney Michelle Brooker was so easy to talk to because she trulely cared about my family and I. In the courtroom she was very professional and assertive and I was very pleased she was who I chose to represent me and my family. My exes attorney was an unprofessional joke. Please always do your research when hiring an attorney. I'm happy I did mine and came across Attorney Brooker. I would recommend her 100% and hire her to represent me and my family again without any hesitation.
Shaun Hanson is the most professional, honorable, and knowledgeable attorney I've ever spoken with..........the best

This law firm is a complete waste of time and money. They offered zero guidance and hand you over to a paralegal immediately.  Look elsewhere and save yourself time, money and hassle. In a no-pressure, slow divorce they charged me almost $1000 for "Checking-in" every few months on a file that was basically on hold. If you like paying $50 to receive a one-sentence email and $50 then for them to review your one-sentence reply then this law firm is the place for you!  It's not really ethical to milk your clients retainer for no good reason and just because it's there. If there's blood in the water the sharks are circling. Thanks for nothing!

I've had quite a few attorneys in this town for two different custody cases. The first I dropped after I was charged over $4k in one month without a shred of work to show for the charges (not even a response), another I left when I switched over to HGBH and she told me in a phone call that Shaun Hanson is the only attorney in this town she didn't mind losing a client to. Shaun took on my move away case with the utmost confidence that it would be granted and I am so thankful for all the hard work he and his team put into my case. We were in H2 with Judge Warren and for starters I have to say, when you see a judge happy to have your attorney in the courtroom you know you made the right decision. Shaun represented me at two court dates and then Erik Bradford carried my case through four very long days of trial. Both men were prepared and kept the entire case simple. They did not prolong the process the way  my ex-husband's attorney tried to. They simply worked based on the case law regarding move aways and I can say without a shadow of a doubt that it made it all that much easier for the judge to grant it.

Monica worked as the paralegal on my case and she was incredibly responsive and always on top of the documentation/paperwork in both a timely and efficient manner. She was always receptive to my phone calls and treated me like a human. She never overbilled nor billed the way I saw other paralegals bill in this town.

I have recommended HGBH to friends, family, and even strangers. If I could put this entire law office in my pocket and take them with me to OK when we move, I would. I would like to say you get what you pay for but like I mentioned above, I have paid a sickening amount of money for "reviewing case file", "updating case file", etc with nothing to show for it. Do your research, go in for a consultation, feel the vibes in the office. From the first day I stepped into the office at HGBH I could feel the integrity of the entire staff and it was evident that they are passionate about their clients needs. I see other comments here that the office is over prices or only interested in money but I can tell you from personal experience that is far from the truth. Be mindful of your billing, if you are calling everyday you will get charged, its not rocket science. They are a law office and if you take up too much of their time for the same request you will likely be billed, however, if you are mindful and trust them Id be willing to be you will have the outcome you desire which includes your billing.
You get what you pay for! They are the best. The outcome was better than we could’ve ever hoped for. Don’t hesitate to use one of the partners at their firm

I highly recommend Mr. Shaun Hanson at Hanson, Gorian, and Bradford Law firm in Murrieta, CA.
Mr. Hanson is honest, trustworthy and very straight forward. In unvarnished truth, he will tell you exactly where you stand and what direction you should go. He has tenacity of tenderness but never losing sight of the end goal, which is to protect, defend and have the best outcome for his client.  He is well respected in the community and has earned this respect following the values of his faith and commitment to his clients he serves that are needing his help.  I went to Mr. Hanson because of a recommendation from a friend when my ex-husband decided that he wanted full custody of my daughter who was entering into middle school (we had 50/50 visitation). Mr. Hanson was brilliant in his representation of me and my daughter Jazmyne. He is very intelligent and was forceful when the situation called for it (court hearings or negotiations), but is also very thoughtful and compassionate. I believe that he truly understood my issue and the intricacies involved. Because of his dynamic and powerful representation of us, I was awarded full custody of my daughter, Jazmyne.  I highly recommend Mr. Hanson, although, he is very busy, and in high demand, he is definitely well worth it! Mr. Hanson Staff is exceptional (Dana and Araceli) They always provided me with a piece of mind when Mr. Hanson wasn't available to speak to at that moment.  They were always able to answer any of my questions. You won't be disappointed. I'm forever grateful, we're forever grateful! You gave my daughter a chance to be a kid again and experience all that she could be involved in school/after school activities. Blessings to you all 🙏🏽

This attorney firm is by far the worst I have ever used. I went to them in hopes to be the voice for my daughter!! Not the case!! They were only about the money!  They were very unprepared and met with my daughter 10 before court!! We just wanted them to be her voice. She is very shy and was in a horrible situation with her little boys father. I knew it would cost money and didn’t mind paying. They did nothing!!!! If you don’t mind spending money and getting nothing done then this is the law firm for you!! But if I were you check out the reviews on yelp and google. Don’t waste you time!!
Awesome and efficient

Shawn was unprofessional, thinks he's the best lawyer but couldn't win a simple case were it was in our favor. Lacks knowledge of the law, don't waste your $400 dollars in hour with this amateur of a lawyer. Only interested in your money.

Went in to talk to an attorney for a free consultation. The receptionist comfirmed that I could see the attorney right then. I filled out the form and she took it back. A paralegal came out 5 min later telling me that he was to busy to see me, but offered the nane and number of another attorney down the road. I went to him cause he already knew 1/2 of my situation other than that I would have went to another attorney. They kept over charging me, and I was sent to other attorneys for my last case. I paid attorney fees and hardly seen the attorney, just someone that could tell me what to say during my court date. Very knowledgeable, but a rip off.
Not a good experience.

reviews.authorName: Matt McDonald (Down Below the Up Above)

reviews.text: Mr. Hanson was all that I could ask for in a divorce attorney and more. I felt confident walking into each hearing and am more than thankful to have had this office behind me during this trying time. I would strongly recommend Mr. Hanson to anyone going through a divorce or custody matter.

I'm beyond confused as to why or even how there could be bad reviews for this firm.  I Personally couldn't say enough about how truly amazing Attorney Hanson and his office where in handling my case. No one wants to be in a situation where you need to hire a family attorney, it's very overwhelming and emotional, and not something you should attempt to do on your own. I originally tried to handle my divorce pro per, it didn't take long before my ex was using the divorce as a way to further bully and manipulate me. By the time I realized I couldn't handle this on my own, my ex had total control over the divorce and everything was for his benefit, on his terms and in his favor. Hiring this firm saved me, everyone that I met was so very considerate and understanding! As crazy as it sounds, having a firm of truly remarkable individuals on your side can save your sanity! I don't know what I would have done without them, I sincerely credit them with saving me. I was able to walk away from my divorce happy, not scared as I originally thought I would be when I was handling it myself. There's not enough words to express my appreciation to Attorney Hanson and his staff, thank you for treating me and my case so well!

With that being said all I can say is I wouldn't hesitate for a second to recommend Shaun Hanson to my friends and family. This firm will be your life saver, it was for me! Don't go through something so hard by yourself!

This office, makes false promises and falsely represent's what they stand for. They are over priced, and are not family friendly. They are not passionate about what they say they stand for. Beware, no refunds for lazy attorneys.

I really believed they were going to help with my case.  The attorney's all look so professional and sure of themselves but in the end all they care about is money charging for everything.  Sometimes I wouldn't get phone calls back but i was still being  charged for speaking with no one.  Months and my situation gotten worse instead of better no point paying for attorney's if they dont care about their clients.
Handled my matter professionally and promptly. A+
I called looking for a sense of direction with my felony and Bryce called back with terrific news. The response time and professionalism was amazing.

I called for some advice as to whether i need legal help or not and Mr. Amendariz explained so much in a short time without making it seem like i needed to hire him in order for things to go my way. Very very helpful, if i do pursue any actions i will be sure to have him in my corner

Extremely professional and amazing law firm. They help you with every step of the way and make sure you taken care of. I will definitely be staying in contact with this firm if I ever come against another situation.

Without a doubt, my family HIGHLY recommends the Armendariz Law Firm.  My 23 year old son was facing a felony assault charge with a GBI enhancement; a 7 year jail sentence.  He had never been in trouble with the law other than 2 moving violations since he was 17.  The "victim" had a black eye which didn't require hospitalization or medical follow up so nothing was broken.  We had never sought or were in need of a criminal defense attorney.  This was all new to us.  Prior to coming across the Armendariz Law Firm, my son and I had met with another attorney who didn't quite win us over, but it wasn't bad either.  I had three other appointments for my son after setting up the appointment with Bryce Armendariz.  I could not attend my son's appointment.  When I called my son, he was excited.  He felt immediately connected to Bryce.  Bryce asked the right questions of my son.  His fee was quite a bit higher than that of the first attorney, but when you're facing such a serious charge, the difference was a minuscule.  We cancelled all subsequent appointments and put all our trust into Bryce.  We were not disappointed! Bryce did an AMAZING job!  He communicated the entire way through with us, he was present for every hearing and really assisted us through the entire process.  The GBI enhancement was removed. My son ended up with 90 days of electronic monitoring, 16 classes of Anger Management and restitution of under $1,500, made up of mostly court fees.  He's able to downgrade from a felony to a misdemeanor after a year AND it is eligible for expungement after his 3 year probation is up.  We plan on using Bryce's services for these future engagements.  Thank you Bryce for your ethics, your knowledge, your communication, and your honesty.  Without you, I don't think we would have had the same outcome.

Bryce was great with keeping us informed with every aspect of our case. His answers to our many questions were always clear and complete. He made sure we understood everything before proceeding with anything. We highly recommend this law firm, we promise you will be in good hands.

I hired this lawyer after a car accident because I believed he was the lawyey who would properly fight for my case. I clearly explained everything that happened, and he told me he would take the case. He then assigned his case manager, Sarah, but communication was very poor. She rarely responded, and the attorney himself was always busy. Because of this, I lost a lot of valuable time.
I needed surgery and help finding a doctor, but he did not help me at all. I felt completely unsupported during a very stressful situation. In the end, he dropped my case because it seemed he only wanted something easy. All he did was file a claim with my own insurance, which I could have done myself.
Later, I found out that he was not actually a lawyer as I initially believed, but an attorney who did not fight for my case or protect my interests. This was very disappointing. He wasted my time and energy, and I do not recommend him to anyone who needs strong legal representation.
Abe made a difficult situation much easier. He explained everything clearly, stayed in touch, and delivered a great result. I wouldn’t hesitate to recommend him he’s the best in the game

I’m really grateful for the help I got from my lawyer Abe after my car accident. From the start, he was proactive, responsive, and genuinely cared about what I was going through. He took the time to explain everything in a way that made sense and always answered my questions, which made a stressful situation feel a lot less stressful.

He handled all the back and forth and negotiations so I could focus on recovering, and in the end, he helped me get a settlement I was really happy with. I always felt supported and I knew he had my best interests in mind.

If you’re looking for a lawyer who’s professional and truly has your back, I’d definitely recommend Abe. I wouldn’t hesitate to send friends or family his way and in fact I already have, and will continue to do so. Thanks Abe for everything! I’m really grateful I have you as my lawyer.

I had the pleasure of having Ibrahim represent me and my wife in several cases.
He is a fighter. Very meticulous and knowledgeable.
He won all of our cases.
Above all he is very honest and trustworthy.
Bashir Alghizawi
Enjoyed my entire process. Made me feel comfortable and confident.
reviews.ownerResponse: Hi Chef Kelz- thank you so much! I’m really glad to hear you enjoyed the entire process. It means a lot to know you felt comfortable and confident throughout. I appreciate your kind words and look forward to working with you again!

reviews.text: I appreciate the diligence of this office when fighting my case. The “at fault” insurance tried to offer me and my husband an insanely low amount for the damages and trauma we had experienced from our car accident. This legal team was able to negotiate 5x the initial amount offered. I am thankful for their work and willingness to fight the case, despite the other party’s initial low offer.
reviews.ownerResponse: Hi E- thank you so much for your kind words and for trusting our team to handle your case. We appreciate you taking the time to share your experience! Achieving a result that better reflected the damages and trauma you experienced was very important to us. Your patience and trust throughout the process meant a great deal to our team. We’re grateful for the opportunity to fight on your behalf and wish you both all the best moving forward.

reviews.text: I’m so grateful for The Accident Guys for taking on my case! They worked hard to get the medical attention I needed and to seek out the highest payout possible for my recovery. I was updated throughout the entire process and even after the case was settled I was provided with information about my payout and was asked how I felt about their services which shows how much they value their clients! These images reflect my accident and my traumatic experience. Thank you to Ariane for keeping me updated  and seeking the best places for medical attention (chiropractic care) and thank you to Karla for reaching out to me once the case was settled to make sure I was content with the outcome!
reviews.ownerResponse: Hi Angelique- thank you so much for taking the time to share this! It means a lot to us. We’re incredibly grateful you trusted The Accident Guys with your case, and we’re glad we could help you get the medical care and support you needed during such a difficult time. Keeping our clients informed and heard is really important to us, so it’s wonderful to know that made a difference for you. Ariane and Karla will be so happy to hear your kind words. We wish you continued healing and the very best moving forward.
Thank you for the 5-star, Ed!

reviews.text:
Reviews helped me when I was searching for an attorney, so I’m returning the favor!  I would 100% recommend The Accident Guys.  I had guidance, support & someone I could get in touch with, throughout the entire process.  I was at a complete stop on the 15 North, in Temecula, when a car behind me didn’t realize traffic had stopped.  In the fast lane.  It’s been a long, very hard road to healing.  I was blessed to have chosen the right firm to help me on this overwhelming journey.  Marcelo was at my house the day after I called with questions.  Carla & Martha helped me get in touch with the right medical doctors and mental health care.  Acie helped get things wrapped up and even got me a financial advance when I was in a pinch.  Attorney Masha was there throughout the process.  Everyone a phone call or text away.  It’s been 19 months and they never stopped fighting for me.  My well being and healing, always their focus.  Of course the financial aspect as well; we won.  They fought for me and won for me!    I’m sure the entire firm is amazing, but if I had a loved one who needed someone on their side, I would tell them to ask for Masha & her team, at The Accident Guys.  I honestly will miss talking to them.  lol. They were there for a few emotional breakdowns along the way.  lol. Thank you all so much for being an answered prayer.  May all that you give, come back to you.
Blessings always,
Julie

Julie, thank you for such a thoughtful and heartfelt review. We’re so sorry for everything you went through—it sounds like an incredibly difficult experience—but we’re honored to have walked alongside you during such a tough chapter. Our family had gotten into a bad car accident while sitting at a stop light an SUV rear ended us at full force because the driver dropped something and took her eyes off the road and didn't realize it was a red light, she rammed us at full force. Immediately after the car accident with a family member's suggestion on seeking out a lawyer. I went ahead and searched for local lawyers and found The Accident Guys in reading their reviews I went ahead a contacted them.

The initial process was very quick and a lawyer contacted us that very same day, as soon as the next day we were getting treated for our injuries. We were assigned a caseworker named Dana, she was very helpful and friendly but the process seemed to slow down at point within a month of treatments, we needed MRIs and other pain management treatments but it was taking 6 weeks and more at a time to get approvals from their offices, we finally just decided to close our case to put it behind us. Joseph took over our case to close it, he was a huge help to us in handling our case, it was not a quick process but it was a lot more timely than getting the treatment approvals from Dana our case manager. Accident Guys are helpful in settling your accident case, I would recommend them to others but will also let people know it is a process and not to expect things to happen quickly.

Thanks to Andrew’s skill and dedication, we were able to win my cases and achieve an outcome I was hoping for. I truly felt like I had someone in my corner who cared and knew exactly how to navigate the legal system to my advantage.
If you’re looking for a family law attorney who is not only highly skilled but also genuinely committed to his clients’ best interests, I would absolutely recommend Andrew Rosenberry and his team at Antonyan Miranda.

I was in a really difficult situation with custody, I was searching and speaking with many top lawyers in San Diego for the help I was looking for, I did not feel good about any of them. I was then referred to Antonyan Miranda and I went into a consultation with a few of their lawyers, I needed to try all options. Andrew Rosenberry totally understood my needs, he made me feel hope. He was determined and followed through with his promises!! It is so rare to have a person follow through with persistence like that!! I am literally so grateful I can’t even write words that express it. Their prices are steep, but it is worth because the end justifies the means. Andrew is the real deal, he was meant to help me and my children. I highly recommend this firm!
Direct, no nonsense legal team—Exactly what I was looking for. I don’t have money or time to waste on extra legal fees and they exceeded my expectations getting the job done!

Quoted me $20,000 to finalize my divorce that was filed a year ago. We are retired, our children are grown and we have our home on the market wherein we’ve agreed to split the proceeds 50/50. What a whack job. No other attorney has ever quoted me that much. Run away from this law firm.
